history.outcome:
Everyone was super keen: clinical staff, Dr Mike Whitaker (overseeing recall across OneMedical) and Jess (executing recall in Brooklands and Whitehouse). Jess tried recalling 1 patient and loved the results, Dr Whitaker just asked her to hold off on full recall until July so he has time to secure OneMedical exec approval. Jess also preferred this because she wants to work with a list of clean July patients due to horrific missing coding Apr-June.

Amy to see Mike and Jess again at Whitehouse on 29 June, and then follow up with Mike mid-July re a full rollout across OneMedical :)

history.outcome:
• Love it, no blocking issues.
- Entire admin team (10-20 staff) trained on it and 100+ patients recalled.
- Testimonial to be recorded later due to being shy in office!

Followup:
  • Share new releases in WhatsApp group, Cydney really wants this visibility.
  • TQUEST FAILED END OF MAY. Logging inserted and chased to restart recall on 16 June. Cydney restarted recall.
  • Under 18s excluded from recall 30 June, just in time for their 2000 recall commercial conversation on 1 July.

history.outcome:
• Love it, no blocking issues.
- Called Ed from Wickham Surgery straight away to "ask him why the hell he's not on this yet”, he said he’ll visit their practice to see.
- Testimonial video and “beat the clock” videos captured.

More quotes:
- How has NHS England not picked up on this? It should be rolled out everywhere! They should invest in this for the entire general practice, it’s incredible. This is the difference between needing improvement and being good in CQC. I feel like this is ChatGPT for recall. I’m going to be redundant!

They said on a call on 25 June that they don’t use it for ACT questionnaires yet and want to try it in a demo environment first. Amy made and sent them a help guide with screenshots and hope that helps.
history.problems:
Non-blocking:
-  Mandatory location field means they need to check patients’ address in EMIS and then manually change the location one by one.
- Hampshire Lab setting is ‘Collection at hospital’ but it should be ‘I want to “Request Now” and “Collect Sample Later”’: fixed
- Suggestion logic is inaccurate: wrongly picking up topical corticosteroids + aren’t picking up anyone in their PSA search: fixed
- Investigations aren’t coding QOF codes yet: fixed
- They need to edit every SMS with specific instructions to ring reception for certain patient subsets: fixed
- They would edit SMSs to “Dear Mr/Mrs. Surname” and simplifying the indication list to “diabetes”, “cardiovascular health”, “respiratory health” and “medication”: fixed
- They want QOF codes removed for smoking status questionnaire

• Love it, no blocking issues.
- Called Ed from Wickham Surgery straight away to "ask him why the hell he's not on this yet”, he said he’ll visit their practice to see.
- Testimonial video and “beat the clock” videos captured.

More quotes:
- How has NHS England not picked up on this? It should be rolled out everywhere! They should invest in this for the entire general practice, it’s incredible. This is the difference between needing improvement and being good in CQC. I feel like this is ChatGPT for recall. I’m going to be redundant!

They said on a call on 25 June that they don’t use it for ACT questionnaires yet and want to try it in a demo environment first. Amy made and sent them a help guide with screenshots and hope that helps.

- Hampshire Lab setting is ‘Collection at hospital’ but it should be ‘I want to “Request Now” and “Collect Sample Later”’: fixed
- Suggestion logic is inaccurate: wrongly picking up topical corticosteroids + aren’t picking up anyone in their PSA search: fixed
- Investigations aren’t coding QOF codes yet: fixed
- They need to edit every SMS with specific instructions to ring reception for certain patient subsets: fixed
- They would edit SMSs to “Dear Mr/Mrs. Surname” and simplifying the indication list to “diabetes”, “cardiovascular health”, “respiratory health” and “medication”: fixed
- They want QOF codes removed for smoking status questionnaire

J82218.outcome:
- Entire practice was there, they love it in theory, it “went down a storm” but need to still do the recalling side-by-side session another time.
- Followup: had 1:1 sessions with Gemma and told her full target data was ready for recall on 1 June. Chased again on 8 June.
J82218.problems:
• Blocking: booking links showing no availability even though can see there are 300+ appts free

- Remove exclamation mark in SMS: solved by 23 April
- Don’t allow us to sync all target data: solved 01 June
  • Want multiple appointments: solved
  • Second automated recall message goes out too soon after the first; Gemma feels like it overwhelms patients. Would prefer it to be one month.
  • Removal of name in SMS means HCAs can no longer tell who’s been recalled by Gemma and who is with Suvera (although should be able to tell by the Planner tiles?)

history.outcome:
Video testimonial in exchange for adding ad hoc bloods in Planner :D

Additional written quote:
“You’ve no idea how much you’ve made my life easier. I feel like I’m cheating. It’s so much fun: just click to say “yes” and everything’s done for you! I’ve no idea why no one’s thought of this before.”
history.problems:
Non-blocking:
  • Want to add bloods ad hoc for patients to opportunistically add for health checks: to solve
  • Still want to SMS/email housebound patients to recall in Planner, just with different appointment rules (visits). Questionnaires and contact channels stay the same.
  • Optimise booking links for multiple appointments when patients need AST/COPD: Amy to solve
  • work being duplicated in VC as Suvera care team does not have the same visibility as the practice of what bloods have been requested

history.outcome: Ben/#ehr-support to investigate and get back to Salma on WhatsApp: Amy messaged her same-day. Salma to continue playing and recalling anyway but no reply. Amy checked in on WhatsApp 25 June; Salma called Amy on 29 June to request delaying the second invite by 4 weeks. Emphasised big engagement issues in her population which means she spaces out attempts monthly throughout the year + avg. 6 attempts per patient. Amy helped her with re-engaging patients with Suvera which she is happy to try in the meantime. Need to check in July.
history.problems:
Nonblocking:
  • UCLP risk bug, top patient shouldn’t be high risk: solved same-day.
  • HYP target bug, patient was excepted from target when only exception coded in 2019: solved same-day
  • Wants to delay second invite by 4 weeks to give patients enough time to book

history.outcome: They filmed a testimonial, presented it at their PCN meeting, asked for their entire team to get accounts… and then haven’t used it again! Upon Amy chasing on 3 June, Lindsay said they’ve had to postpone team training due to sickness and hope to get going in the next couple weeks.
history.problems:
Non-blocking:
  • Remove “Andrew” from the signature: solved 17 June not notified yet
  • Edit to add unrelated bloods and questionnaires
  • Allow multiple (but not all) practitioners to be selected in a booking link
  • want to be able to add more bloods to the standard recalls; their practice requests the following for ALL their LTC recalls: FBC, U&Es, LFTs, HbA1c, Lipids, TFTs. this is blocking them from bulk recalling in large numbers as they have to manually request these additional bloods for the majority of patients. (asking Andrew to clarify clinical reason for this; may be that they can do away with requesting extra bloods and just trust Planner)

history.outcome: Ton of blocking requests, to the point it’s not worth optimising for them. We will do whatever is mutually beneficial to other practices too.
history.problems:
Blocking:  
  • Needs to send blood forms to patients - their site is super far from hospital and where some patients live.
  • Rename surgery to “The Vale Medical Practice”
  • S1 bulk for daily syncs + booking links
  • Would like to sign off as Dr Shah instead of Hetul
  • Needs bloods results to go to a fake doctor not their named GP because they have inaccurately categorised patients’ named GPs in S1. 
• Needs FBC, HbA1c, uACR, LFTs, lipids for everyone on LTC register.
  • Does not want magnesium for digoxin.
  • Local prescribing incentive scheme (PIS) target is missing?

Non-blocking:
  • Would like to identify missing codes.
  • Needs to add extra bloods ad hoc for acute, NHS health checks etc.
  • Would like to find patients who have submitted very high BP and have been seen but are suboptimally controlled.
  •  Would like ICE to select “high risk of infection” depending on the patient’s conditions.
  • Would like ICE to select category “GP NHS”

history.outcome: Successful test recall! Nicole and David love it. Mike Whitaker asked them to hold off more recalls until all OneMedical practices have been set up and they can go live at once. Amy to follow up with Mike mid-July.
history.problems:
Non-blocking:
  • Want S1 booking links. Appt config:
Bloods only = “Bloods”
Bloods + footcheck or footcheck only = “Initial assessment”
DM/NDH review = “Diabetic telephone reviews”
Asthma review = “Asthma telephone reviews”
Asthma + COPD or COPD only = “COPD”
Any other review $nin asthma + COPD + SMI + SMR = “Medication review telephone”
Any other single or multimorb review $nin asthma + COPD = ”SMI/SMR/Complex Med review”
history.site_address: The Headrow, Balcony Level, Leeds LS1 8TL

history.outcome: Confirm go-live date with OneMedical execs before end of July.
history.problems:
• before booking links are available for S1, how does Planner know if a pt has booked in for an appt? i.e. how will it know to not send a follow up SMS?
• asked if they can see case-finding opportunities (exploring in missed coding them); also keen for automated recall 
• follow up SMS after 7 days is fine if syncs are daily, but if its 4+ days then pts might get a 2nd recall text even if they’ve actioned their tasks – they currently leave one month between first and second recall e.g. if someone’s on holiday
• asked about vaccinations and imms
